files.name: sample.java

vulnerabilities.description: The method `testDomain()` contains a dangerous call `Process process = Runtime.getRuntime().exec(...);` at line 25 that processes data without adequate security controls.
vulnerabilities.impact:
An attacker can execute arbitrary OS commands on the server by using shell metacharacters (" ; & | () $ > < 
). This may compromise system integrity or access unauthorized resources.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Avoid shell command execution entirely. Use HttpURLConnection for HTTP requests instead of invoking "curl" in a subshell.

vulnerabilities.description: [Exploitable] The method `testDomain()` contains a dangerous call `Process process = Runtime.getRuntime().exec("sh", "-c", "ping -c 1"+domainName));` at line 40 that processes data without adequate security controls.
vulnerabilities.impact: An attacker can execute arbitrary OS commands on the server, potentially achieving full system compromise including data exfiltration, lateral movement, and persistent backdoor installation.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Avoid shell command execution entirely. Use Java APIs directly (e.g., HttpURLConnection for HTTP requests). If shell invocation is unavoidable, use ProcessBuilder with a fixed command array (no shell expansion) as argument.

vulnerabilities.description: 'request' is tainted by source 'unknown', flow: user -> server request -> method `GET /api?filePath=$FILE://etc//passwd HTTP/1.1` at row 4.
vulnerabilities.impact: Exploitation of this Tainted Input Channel can lead to injection attacks on databases through SQL queries ('SQL Injection'), command execution via OS commands ('Command Injection') or remote file inclusion ('Remote Code Execution'). This channel may be used for XSS attack vectors as well due to its incorporation into HTML output without proper sanitization.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Review all input channels that feed data back into dangerous functions like eval(), exec() etc., ensuring they have appropriate validation filters applied.

vulnerabilities.description: The method `testWebsite()` contains a dangerous call `this.rest.e... at line 14 that processes data without adequate security controls.
vulnerabilities.impact: An attacker can exploit XXE to read arbitrary files on the server by sending crafted XML input through POST parameters or SSRF payloads while performing external requests like GET /api/endpoint?paramXmlFileUrl=file://etc//passwd&xxeparams[]=%3Canimate+xml+external+entity+attack -H "Content-Type: application/json" --data '{"field": {"@value": "/pathToXxeAttackerScript/"}', which could lead to unauthorized access to sensitive system directories such as config/, credential databases (* .env), source codes (.git/) etc., potentially achieving full server compromise including lateral movement across different services running under similar user IDs due to misconfigured file permissions.
vulnerabilities.recommendation:
Review all calls containing raw user input for reflected XSS attacks using OWASP ESAPI Encoder.
Validate URL schemes against allowlist of http:/ https:/ dat:// about:/.
